## 2026-04-10 Session

### Prep

- [x] **Follow-through:** Marcus started the fallback design
- [x] **Carry-forward:** Slipping commitments pattern — check in
- [x] **Pending feedback:** Estimation discussion (carried from Feb 5)
- [x] **Recent work:** Phoenix is still blocked on infra team

### Notes

**Discussion:**
- Marcus brought a written fallback ingestion design to the 1:1. Clean structure. Realistic estimates. Called out his own risks.
- Went back to the Phoenix blocker review meeting from April 7 — he said he "froze" when the scope cut came up. Asked why. Said it felt like his failure, and he didn't know what to say.
- Reframed the scope cut as a reasonable engineering decision, not a concession. Landed well.
- Parental leave: filed this morning. Start May 18, 12 weeks. HR has a process.
- Talked about what he wants coverage to look like — he suggested Nate as a co-owner. Need to think about that.

**Action Items:**
- Marcus to finalize fallback design doc by April 14
- You to draft parental leave coverage plan with HR
- You to think about whether Nate is the right co-owner for Phoenix

**Decisions:**
- None yet on coverage
